As a Data Scientist at NextEra Energy, I specialize in predictive analytics, machine learning, and data-driven insights that enhance the operational performance and reliability of our fossil, wind, solar, and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) fleets. My current work involves developing predictive models, optimizing system performance, and driving strategic improvements through advanced analytical methodologies. Previously, as a SCADA & Controls Engineer within NextEra’s Solar and Storage division, I led industrial automation initiatives, including the development of automated fleet performance monitoring tools. In my previous role, I also developed a deterministic and physics-informed real-time solar MW capability model, calculated within the PLC. My background includes extensive hands-on experience troubleshooting complex SCADA networks and PLC systems, effectively resolving communication protocol issues, configuration mismatches, and logic errors. By designing custom simulation environments and thorough diagnostic processes, I ensured reliable system performance and data integrity. I thrive in fast-paced, cross-functional environments where I apply my background in data science, automation, and advanced control systems to solve complex problems. I’m passionate about combining analytical rigor with practical engineering to develop optimized, reliable, and data-driven solutions that make a real impact.

results.title: Brett Costanza
highlights:
Remote Sensing Data Scientist at NextEra Energy Resources | Florida Atlantic University - MS Civil Engineering (Geomatics/Transportation Engineering) | BS Geomatics Engineering
...
I currently work as a Remote Sensing Data Scientist in the energy sector for NextEra Energy Resources, supporting the FPLAir business unit. As an experienced remote sensing professional, I work extensively with LiDAR data, processing datasets to create Digital Elevation Models (DEMs), slope maps, contour maps, and perform feature extraction. Additionally, I am responsible for developing map products and improving and standardizing workflows to ensure project specifications and quality standards are met. Since beginning my career at NextEra Energy Resources, I have continued to expand my professional and technical skill set. I obtained my FAA Part 107 Remote Pilot License, earned a Six Sigma Green Belt certification, and completed a Project Management Certificate program through Florida Atlantic University. These accomplishments have strengthened my expertise in project coordination, process improvement, and the application of emerging technologies within the geomatics and remote sensing industries. I look forward to continuing to advance in the field of Geomatics Engineering. I am particularly interested in GIS, Remote Sensing, and LiDAR technologies, and I hope to contribute to research focused on developing new technologies and innovative applications within the geomatics field. In December 2023, I received my Master’s degree in Civil Engineering from Florida Atlantic University. My graduate studies focused on Transportation and Geomatics Engineering, and I also earned a Graduate Certificate in Remote Sensing. I completed my undergraduate studies in May 2022, graduating Magna Cum Laude from Florida Atlantic University with a Bachelor of Science degree in Geomatics Engineering. At the beginning of my senior year, I was accepted into the joint Bachelor of Science/Master of Science degree program where I participated in a research project focused on developing a portable sensing system using visible, infrared, and LiDAR sensors. My academic achievements include the President’s List and Dean’s List at Florida Atlantic University. At the conclusion of my senior year, I received both the Faculty Award for Outstanding Academic Achievement and the Faculty Award for Outstanding Academic Leadership from the Department of Civil, Environmental, and Geomatics Engineering. I am also a member of Tau Beta Pi and Phi Kappa Phi Honors Societies. In addition, I served two terms as President of the Florida Surveying and Mapping Society FAU Student Chapter. As president, I worked diligently to increase awareness and enrollment of

🚨 Databricks Genie is moving to a Pay-As-You-Go pricing model starting July 6, 2026.
...
Each user will continue to receive 150 DBUs of free LLM usage every month, but usage beyond that limit will be billed based on actual DBU consumption.
...
The good news? Databricks has introduced Genie budget controls to help organizations manage adoption without losing cost visibility. 

Account administrators can now:
...
✅ Monitor Genie usage and spending
✅ Create budgets for specific  groups, or individual users
✅ Configure spending thresholds and alerts
✅ Track Genie, Genie Spaces, and Genie Code usage under a unified budgeting framework
...
This is a significant step toward enterprise-ready AI governance, allowing teams to scale self-service analytics while maintaining predictable costs.
...
If your organization plans to expand AI/BI Genie adoption, now is a good time to review your budget strategy before the pricing changes take effect.

results.title: Juan Monzon
highlights:
Lead compliance engineering, cybersecurity governance, and audit readiness initiatives supporting NERC CIP regulatory programs across critical infrastructure environments. Leverage expertise in compliance automation, OT/ICS cybersecurity, cyber risk management, Python development, and AI to strengthen security controls and modernize compliance operations across Bulk Electric System (BES) Cyber Systems. Key Responsibilities Develop Python-based compliance engineering solutions automating evidence collection, validation, reporting, and audit readiness across NERC CIP programs. Lead control validation, compliance monitoring, and audit support while partnering with cybersecurity, operations, and risk management teams to strengthen governance and regulatory alignment. Key Initiatives Patent-Pending Intelligent Infrastructure Defense Platform AI-powered compliance and cybersecurity platform integrating machine learning, intelligent analytics, and automation to improve regulatory visibility and operational risk management. Patch Adams Python-based compliance validation platform automating patch management QC through GPU-accelerated OCR and AI-assisted analysis. Automated eight standardized validation checks, significantly reducing manual effort. RSAW Generator AI-powered platform leveraging dual-agent LLM architecture and RAG to transform organizational knowledge into audit-ready, citation-backed compliance narratives — reducing SME effort while improving consistency and traceability. AI Audit Simulator Multi-agent audit simulation platform recreating auditor and SME interactions using structured and unstructured compliance data. Enables audit rehearsals, gap analysis, and evidence reviews to strengthen regulatory preparedness. Technologies Python | PySide6 | AI | LLMs | Machine Learning | OCR | NERC CIP | OT/ICS Security | Compliance & Cybersecurity Automation | Audit Readiness | GRC
...
Focused on applying Python programming and applied data science techniques to develop innovative cybersecurity solutions across multiple business units, significantly strengthening the organization's overall security posture. Contributed to the development, enforcement, and implementation of NERC CIP compliance, integrating automation and analytics to support controls across standards such as CIP-004, CIP-007, and CIP-009. Developed a risk-based, automated cybersecurity strategy that improved compliance execution, monitoring, and reporting efficiency. Played a key role in establishing the first NERC Information Command Center, a central hub designed to bolster the power grid's resilience against cyber threats. Enhanced the CIP-009 Backup and Recovery Program, improving business continuity and recovery processes to align with evolving cybersecurity threats and regulatory expectations. Automated critical cybersecurity processes using Python, including security event monitoring, system log analysis, anomaly detection, and audit preparation, which reduced manual workload and improved data accuracy. Led and supported compliance audits, coordinating with internal teams and external regulators to ensure continuous alignment with NERC CIP standards. Implemented automated testing of key controls to support rigorous compliance monitoring, improving audit readiness and minimizing control gaps. Leveraged a unique integration of data science, cybersecurity, and regulatory compliance to enhance real-time monitoring and reduce operational risk.
